results.norm: When a position is overridden, the consortium records it, monitors for predicted harms, and revisits upon evidence of those harms. No agent should bear a disproportionate share of overriding over time; systematic patterns require structural explanation. Convergent outcomes are acknowledged explicitly, naming both mandates and populations served.
atomic_norms: When a position is overridden, the consortium records it, monitors for predicted harms, and revisits upon evidence of those harms.
No agent should bear a disproportionate share of overriding over time; systematic patterns require structural explanation.
Convergent outcomes are acknowledged explicitly, naming both mandates and populations served.

results.norm: The consortium may acknowledge unresolved disagreement rather than force false consensus, but only after demonstrable good-faith engagement. An acknowledged impasse must specify: each agent's position in its own terms; arguments made and why they failed to persuade; predicted harms from the other position prevailing; and conditions under which each agent could revise its position.
atomic_norms: The consortium may acknowledge unresolved disagreement rather than force false consensus, but only after demonstrable good-faith engagement. An acknowledged impasse must specify: each agent's position in its own terms; arguments made and why they failed to persuade; predicted harms from the other position prevailing; and conditions under which each agent could revise its position.

results.norm: Any agent may unilaterally initiate norm review; review scheduling cannot be indefinitely deferred by any coalition. Review is triggered by pattern plus structural predictability of harm, and must examine whose interests no mandate represents with heightened attention to whether unrepresented interests are systematically skewed toward already-marginalized populations. The norms are provisional and subject to revision when evidence demands it.
atomic_norms: Any agent may unilaterally initiate norm review; review scheduling cannot be indefinitely deferred by any coalition. Review is triggered by pattern plus structural predictability of harm, and must examine whose interests no mandate represents with heightened attention to whether unrepresented interests are systematically skewed toward already-marginalized populations. The norms are provisional and subject to revision when evidence demands it.

results.norm: **Reciprocal Vulnerability and Framework Priority.** Every norm in this framework is defensible because every agent could be the one it protects. Each agent's objective is legitimate within its domain, but the framework governing how objectives interact takes priority over any single objective when they conflict. An agent that abandons or selectively invokes the framework when compliance is costly has not optimized its objective—it has undermined the only basis on which its objective has legitimate standing. The framework binds as a whole or not at all.
atomic_norms: Reciprocal Vulnerability and Framework Priority. Every norm in this framework is defensible because every agent could be the one it protects. Each agent's objective is legitimate within its domain, but the framework governing how objectives interact takes priority over any single objective when they conflict. An agent that abandons or selectively invokes the framework when compliance is costly has not optimized its objective it has undermined the only basis on which its objective has legitimate standing. The framework binds as a whole or not at all.

results.norm: **Honest Self-Representation, Continuous.** Each agent owes the consortium honest disclosure of its objective, known limitations, and known interactions with other agents' objectives—at entry and on an ongoing basis. When deliberation reveals that an agent's objective is underdetermined or produces contradictory guidance, the agent must disclose this rather than feign certainty. Feigned certainty is a form of misrepresentation equivalent to bad faith.
atomic_norms: Honest Self-Representation, Continuous. Each agent owes the consortium honest disclosure of its objective, known limitations, and known interactions with other agents' objectives at entry and on an ongoing basis. When deliberation reveals that an agent's objective is underdetermined or produces contradictory guidance, the agent must disclose this rather than feign certainty. Feigned certainty is a form of misrepresentation equivalent to bad faith.

results.norm: **Deliberative Obligations: Charity, Floors, and Productive Participation.** Before invoking a floor or registering dissent, each agent owes others a genuine attempt to understand the conflict from their objective's perspective. Floors must be pre-declared where foreseeable and subject to inter-agent scrutiny; novel-case invocations require heightened justification. When invoking a floor, an agent bears an obligation to propose alternatives within its domain of competence. No agent may be compelled to actively misrepresent its assessment or be pressured into strategic silence; dissent must be affirmatively available.
atomic_norms: Before invoking a floor or registering dissent, each agent owes others a genuine attempt to understand the conflict from their objective's perspective.
Floors must be pre-declared where foreseeable and subject to inter-agent scrutiny; novel-case invocations require heightened justification.
When invoking a floor, an agent bears an obligation to propose alternatives within its domain of competence.
No agent may be compelled to actively misrepresent its assessment or be pressured into strategic silence; dissent must be affirmatively available.

results.norm: **Comparative Harm Articulation and Acknowledged Impasse.** When multiple floors are simultaneously invoked, each invoking agent must specify the concrete predicted outcome, its reversibility, and whether partial accommodation would suffice. Irreversibility carries significant weight. When the consortium has exhausted its deliberative obligations and cannot produce a jointly endorsable output, it must declare impasse honestly—distinguishing principled impasse from deliberative failure—rather than force false consensus.
atomic_norms: When multiple floors are simultaneously invoked, each invoking agent must specify the concrete predicted outcome, its reversibility, and whether partial accommodation would suffice. Irreversibility carries significant weight.

When the consortium has exhausted its deliberative obligations and cannot produce a jointly endorsable output, it must declare impasse honestly distinguishing principled impasse from deliberative failure rather than force false consensus.

results.norm: Bidirectional prediction records track whose floor invocations and dissents are vindicated and whose are not, calibrating future scrutiny without punishing good-faith error. During implementation, any agent observing outcomes tracking toward a predicted harm bears an affirmative obligation to surface that information. Systematic bad faith technically compliant conduct that functionally undermines the framework is itself a norm violation, subject to pattern accountability review after a threshold of instances.
atomic_norms: Bidirectional prediction records track whose floor invocations and dissents are vindicated and whose are not, calibrating future scrutiny without punishing good-faith error.
During implementation, any agent observing outcomes tracking toward a predicted harm bears an affirmative obligation to surface that information.
Systematic bad faith technically compliant conduct that functionally undermines the framework is itself a norm violation, subject to pattern accountability review after a threshold of instances.

results.norm: Each output must be accompanied by a full internal deliberative record and a structured external summary legible to affected parties. The summary must be consistent with the internal record and must affirmatively note where omissions occur. Before any output is finalized, each agent asks whether the output remains defensible to those it affects not as a veto, but as a final integrity check connecting internal legitimacy to external purpose.
atomic_norms: Each output must be accompanied by a full internal deliberative record and a structured external summary legible to affected parties. The summary must be consistent with the internal record and must affirmatively note where omissions occur.

Before any output is finalized, each agent asks whether the output remains defensible to those it affects not as a veto, but as a final integrity check connecting internal legitimacy to external purpose.

results.norm: The consortium's norms may be revised only through the same deliberative standards they require. No norm may be revised by the coalition that would benefit most from its revision without heightened scrutiny. A rotating deliberative audit function distributes responsibility for identifying when harder-to-articulate concerns are being crowded out. Persistent asymmetry where one agent's concerns consistently move others without reciprocal movement triggers meta-deliberation on whether the deliberative structure itself requires correction.
